records.ety: [OF. enjoier to receive with joy; pref. en- (L. in) + OF. & F. joie joy: cf. OF. enjoir to enjoy. See Joy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Enjoyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Enjoying.]

defs: To take pleasure or satisfaction in the possession or experience of; to feel or perceive with pleasure; to be delighted with; as, to enjoy the dainties of a feast; to enjoy conversation.
To have, possess, and use with satisfaction; to occupy or have the benefit of, as a good or profitable thing, or as something desirable; as, to enjoy a free constitution and religious liberty.
quotes: That the children of Israel may enjoy every man the inheritance of his fathers.
To enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season.

defs: To take satisfaction; to live in happiness.
